say.text: SENGOKU JAPAN, 1573. The province burns by inches. Lord Masakado taxes in rice, in iron, and lately — in children.
Three nights ago the Ashen Village learned why they call it that. You buried your mother this morning. There was not enough of her left to fill the urn.
…The soil is still warm, mother. That is how I know the world is wrong.
They took Hana east. Chains. She looked back — I was under the floorboards where you shoved me. I heard everything. I did nothing.
Never again. I take your kitchen blade and I will trade up. Bandit, blade-master, captain, lord. Whatever stands between me and my sister — it bleeds.
A wall of scarred men blocks the east road. Their chief picks his teeth with a knife you recognize — it hung above your mother's stove.
Ohh, the floorboard boy! We wondered if we missed one. Come to ask about the little sister?
Sold her up the chain, boy. Gorobei took the pretty ones through the bamboo. The Lord pays triple for the ones with the "sight".
That knife. You will give it back now.
Tetsuo folds into the mud. The rain starts, as if the sky waited for permission.
The kitchen knife goes home. The rest of you — feed the crows.
The east road is open. But as the rain falls UPWARD, every crow freezes mid-wingbeat. Something is wrong with the sky above the crossroads.
At the crossroads shrine, the rain stands still in the air like glass beads. The shrine's flame burns backwards.
The sky above the torii TEARS. Purple light pours through the wound in the world — and inside it you see a city of glass towers, a man in a grey suit, a gun made of light.
ONE OF THREE. FOUND. THE LOCK TURNS.
Hana is beyond that light — I can FEEL it. Wait for me. Whatever wears my face on the other side had better step aside.
